briefdescription.para: Maximum length of a patient name string (including null terminator).
Maximum number of vital sign readings stored per patient.
Append a vital sign reading to the patient's history.

detaileddescription.para: The VitalSigns structure is copied by value into the internal array. If the record is already full (reading_count == MAX_READINGS), the record is left unchanged and 0 is returned.

rec
Pointer to an initialised PatientRecord. Must not be NULL.
v
Pointer to the VitalSigns reading to append. Copied by value; the caller may free or reuse v afterwards.
1 if the reading was successfully appended, 0 if the buffer is full.
rec != NULL
v != NULL
On success: rec->reading_count is incremented by 1.
On failure: rec is unchanged.
Requirement
SWR-PAT-002
Append a vital sign reading to the patient's history.
VitalSigns is copied by value (struct assignment), so the caller retains ownership of the original and may reuse the variable.

briefdescription.para: Return the overall alert status derived from the latest reading.

detaileddescription.para: Delegates to overall_alert_level() on the most recent reading. Returns ALERT_NORMAL if no readings have been recorded yet.

rec
Pointer to an initialised PatientRecord. Must not be NULL.
AlertLevel based on the latest VitalSigns, or ALERT_NORMAL if reading_count == 0.
Requirement
SWR-PAT-004
Return the overall alert status derived from the latest reading.
Delegates entirely to overall_alert_level() to keep classification logic centralised in vitals.c — consistent with single-responsibility.

memberdef.argsstring: (PatientRecord *rec, int id, const char *name, int age, float weight_kg, float height_m)
memberdef.name: patient_init

briefdescription.para: Initialise a PatientRecord with demographic data.

detaileddescription.para: Zero-fills the entire structure before populating fields. reading_count is set to 0. The name string is copied with truncation to MAX_NAME_LEN - 1 characters and guaranteed null-termination.

rec
Pointer to the record to initialise. Must not be NULL.
id
Unique patient identifier.
name
Null-terminated patient name string. Must not be NULL.
age
Patient age in years.
weight_kg
Body weight in kilograms.
height_m
Height in metres.
rec != NULL
name != NULL
rec->reading_count == 0
Requirement
SWR-PAT-001
Initialise a PatientRecord with demographic data.

memset(0) ensures that any unused readings array slots contain all-zero VitalSigns, which classify as CRITICAL across all parameters — a deliberately safe default that prevents silent display of uninitialised data.

briefdescription.para: Check whether the patient record's reading buffer is full.

detaileddescription.para:
rec
Pointer to an initialised PatientRecord. Must not be NULL.
Non-zero (true) if reading_count == MAX_READINGS, zero otherwise.
Requirement
SWR-PAT-005
Check whether the patient record's reading buffer is full.

briefdescription.para: Return a pointer to the most recently added vital sign reading.

detaileddescription.para:
rec
Pointer to an initialised PatientRecord. Must not be NULL.
Pointer to the latest VitalSigns entry within rec, or NULL if no readings have been recorded (reading_count == 0).
The returned pointer points into the PatientRecord's internal array. It becomes invalid if rec is destroyed or moved.
Requirement
SWR-PAT-003
Return a pointer to the most recently added vital sign reading.
Index arithmetic: the last valid slot is readings[reading_count - 1].

briefdescription.para: Print a formatted patient summary to stdout.

detaileddescription.para: Outputs demographic data, BMI, the latest vital signs with individual classifications, the overall status, and any active alerts. Output is suitable for a 80-column terminal.

rec
Pointer to an initialised PatientRecord. Must not be NULL.
This function writes to stdout. In unit tests, redirect stdout before calling to keep test output clean.
Requirement
SWR-PAT-006
Print a formatted patient summary to stdout.
Generates alerts inline for the latest reading using generate_alerts(). Output is formatted for an 80-column terminal with box-drawing borders.

briefdescription.para: Patient record management — demographic data and vital sign history.

detaileddescription.para: This module provides the top-level data structure for a monitored patient and the functions to manage their vital sign history. It composes the vitals and alerts modules to provide a complete patient-status API.

sect3.title: Architecture Position
sect3.para: PatientRecord sits at the top of the module hierarchy: patient.h └──vitals.h(classificationofindividualreadings) └──alerts.h(alertrecordgeneration)

sect3.title: Storage Model
sect3.para: Readings are stored in a fixed-size array (MAX_READINGS = 10). Once full, further additions are rejected (patient_add_reading() returns 0). This design avoids dynamic memory allocation, a requirement for safety-critical embedded software under IEC 62304.

simplesect.para: Medical Device Software — IEC 62304 Class B compliant. All rights reserved.
